design_philosophy.core_loop: Traverse nonlinear palace dungeons, defeat tower bosses, collect magic bells, burn bells in sacred flame to unlock final boss encounter
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (ludder currency) balanced against enemy difficulty; invisible dungeon entrances create navigation tension
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Ascending a dark sorcerer's palace as a prince, systematically dismantling magical power by destroying enchanted bells
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Permanent power-up progression (equipment purchases) blurs action-adventure and RPG mechanics; intentionally designed as challenging compared to contemporaries; nonlinear exploration requires hidden room discovery; oblique perspective maze navigation unusual for era

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player weakens enemies indirectly through permanent equipment upgrades purchased with ludder currency; no explicit difficulty settings documented; hidden parallel zones and secret rooms offer alternative power-up sources for resourceful players

progression.upgrade_system: Permanent equipment upgrades purchased with ludder currency from shops; progression from weak starting state (single sword throw) to stronger states through cumulative purchases
progression.unlock_mechanism: Bell collection from seven tower bosses sequentially burns bells in sacred flame to unlock passage to final Rubas encounter; invisible dungeon entrances gate access to main palace
replayability_hooks: Hidden parallel zones containing superior equipment
Secret rooms with alternate power-up locations
Variable shop inventories encourage replaying for specific items

world_building_techniques: Initial dialogue exposition from mysterious kami Khan establishing stakes
Seven-bell plot structure creates thematic narrative progression
Palace and tower architecture reflects sorcerer Rubas's dominion
Sacred flame location serves as narrative anchor point

boss_design.philosophy: Seven tower bosses serve as mandatory progression gates; each boss defeat yields a required story bell; final Rubas boss represents ultimate challenge after bell collection sequence
notable_bosses: Seven tower bosses (individual bells correspond to each)

danger_signals: Khan's warning of monster army summoning
Seven magic bells as weapons of mass destruction
Invisible dungeon entrances suggesting hostile magic
environmental_storytelling: Bell towers as monuments to Rubas's power
Sacred flame location as kingdom's spiritual defense
Hidden parallel zones as corrupted alternate spaces
Shop locations marking safe havens within hostile palace
